# Firestore Database Schema for TaskUp

## Setup Instructions
1. Go to Firebase Console > Firestore Database
2. Click "Create database"
3. Start in **test mode** (or production with rules)
4. Select your location
5. Go to the **Data** tab
6. Click "Add collection" for each collection below

---

## Collection: `zones`

Each user has their own zones for organizing tasks.

**Document ID:** Auto-generated by Firestore

**Fields:**
```
name: string (e.g., "Research", "Homework", "Personal")
color: string (hex code, e.g., "#FF007F", "#FFD500")
userId: string (the Firebase Auth UID of the owner)
createdAt: timestamp
```

**Example Document:**
```
{
  "name": "Research",
  "color": "#FF007F",
  "userId": "abc123xyz...",
  "createdAt": April 15, 2025 at 12:00:00 PM UTC
}
```

---

## Collection: `tasks`

Tasks belong to a user and are organized by zone.

**Document ID:** Auto-generated by Firestore

**Fields:**
```
title: string (required, max 200 chars)
desc: string (optional, task description)
zone: string (zone name, e.g., "Research")
priority: string ("low", "medium", or "high")
dueDateStr: string (ISO format, e.g., "2025-04-20T23:59:00")
reminderStr: string (ISO format for reminder, e.g., "2025-04-20T09:00:00")
calendarDate: string (YYYY-MM-DD format, e.g., "2025-04-20")
completed: boolean (default: false)
completedAt: timestamp (when task was completed)
userId: string (the Firebase Auth UID of the owner)
createdAt: timestamp
updatedAt: timestamp
```

**Example Document:**
```
{
  "title": "Finish research paper",
  "desc": "Chapter 3 needs citations",
  "zone": "Research",
  "priority": "high",
  "dueDateStr": "2025-04-20T23:59:00",
  "reminderStr": "2025-04-20T09:00:00",
  "calendarDate": "2025-04-20",
  "completed": false,
  "completedAt": null,
  "userId": "abc123xyz...",
  "createdAt": April 15, 2025 at 12:00:00 PM UTC,
  "updatedAt": April 15, 2025 at 12:00:00 PM UTC
}
```

---

## Collection: `users`

User profiles with optional college info.

**Document ID:** Use the Firebase Auth UID (set manually)

**Fields:**
```
email: string
displayName: string (optional)
college: string (optional, e.g., "MIT", "Stanford")
createdAt: timestamp
```

**Example Document:**
```
{
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "displayName": "John Doe",
  "college": "MIT",
  "createdAt": April 15, 2025 at 12:00:00 PM UTC
}
```

---

## Firestore Security Rules

Paste this in Firebase Console > Firestore > Rules:

```
rules_version = '2';
service cloud.firestore {
  match /databases/{database}/documents {

    // Helper function to get current user ID
    function getUserId() {
      return request.auth.uid;
    }

    // Helper to check if user is authenticated
    function isAuthenticated() {
      return request.auth != null;
    }

    // Users can read/write their own profile
    match /users/{userId} {
      allow read, write: if isAuthenticated() && request.auth.uid == userId;
    }

    // Users can only access their own zones
    match /zones/{zoneId} {
      allow read, write: if isAuthenticated() && request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId;
      allow create: if isAuthenticated() && request.auth.uid == request.resource.data.userId;
    }

    // Users can only access their own tasks
    match /tasks/{taskId} {
      allow read, update, delete: if isAuthenticated() && request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId;
      allow create: if isAuthenticated() && request.auth.uid == request.resource.data.userId;
    }
  }
}
```

---

## Sample Data (Optional)

After creating the collections, you can add sample zones:

**Sample Zone 1:**
- name: "Research"
- color: "#FF007F"
- userId: (your UID)
- createdAt: (now)

**Sample Zone 2:**
- name: "Homework"
- color: "#FFD500"
- userId: (your UID)
- createdAt: (now)

**Sample Zone 3:**
- name: "Personal"
- color: "#00FF88"
- userId: (your UID)
- createdAt: (now)
